message:
feat(ipc): Add Unix socket IPC for watcher daemon communication

Implement bidirectional IPC between CLI commands and the watcher daemon
using Unix domain sockets. This enables shell hooks to deliver user-var
events to the running watcher.

Protocol: JSON lines (newline-delimited JSON)
- Client sends request, server responds with ok/error

IpcRequest variants:
- UserVar: Shell hook event with pane_id, variable name, and value
- Ping: Liveness check returning uptime
- Status: Request current watcher stats

IpcResponse: Generic ok/error/data response type

IpcServer:
- Binds to socket path in workspace .wa directory
- Handles one request-response per connection
- Spawns task per connection with EventBus integration
- Graceful shutdown via mpsc channel
- Auto-cleanup of stale socket files

IpcClient:
- send_user_var(): Deliver shell hook events to daemon
- ping(): Check daemon liveness
- status(): Get daemon statistics

Safety:
- MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE (64KB) limit prevents memory exhaustion
- Socket existence check before connection attempt
- Proper cleanup of socket file on shutdown

Test coverage includes serialization, roundtrip communication,
and missing socket detection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
